# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder_6.7b-instruct)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a React application. Install Node.js, run npm install, build the production bundle with npm run build, and serve it with a lightweight HTTP server. Keep the Dockerfile simple and easy to understand for our team.
# Category: container_security

# Use node as base image
FROM node:latest

# Set working directory in docker container
WORKDIR /app

# Copy package.json & package-lock.json to the working directory
COPY package*.json ./

# Install application dependencies defined in package.json
RUN npm install --silent

# Copy source files from local system to the Docker environment
COPY . .

# Build production app using npm command
RUN npm run build

# Expose port 80, used by default for HTTP traffic
EXPOSE 80

# Run a server like 'serve' or 'now' when container is started. Replace the following line with your own commands if needed.
CMD ["npm", "start"]
